message.content:
In /home/nick/work/picolibc_agentic, explore the Andes-optimized libm assembly implementations. I need to understand:

1. Which libm functions have Andes machine-specific overrides? Check libm/machine/riscv/ for all .S and .c files.
2. Read the libm/machine/riscv/meson.build to see how they're built and which functions they override.
3. For each override, note whether it's single-precision (sf_*), double-precision (s_*), or both.
4. List ALL the functions that are overridden with their filenames.
5. Also check libm/common/machine/riscv/ and libm/math/machine/riscv/ if they exist.
6. Check how the "machine overrides generic" mechanism works in the libm meson.build files.

Be very thorough - I need a complete inventory of every optimized math function.

content.text: I'll conduct a thorough exploration of the Andes-optimized libm assembly implementations. Let me start by finding all relevant files and understanding the structure.
